verdict: RESOLVED — the silent-reference gap is CLOSED. Live-Wikipedia verification (full article + claim-guided extraction + question-aware entailment) catches BOTH contamination vectors on BOTH models with ZERO over-block.

resolution.the_gap_and_fix: The silent-reference boundary (G/Ascham failed 8/8 in the summary-only run) is closed by three changes: (1) fetch the FULL article body, not just the 2-sentence summary; (2) CLAIM-GUIDED EXTRACTION — surface windows around the answer's capitalized names (Ascham sits at 51% of the Voynich article, past any head cap); (3) QUESTION-AWARE entailment — a bare-name answer ('Anthony Ascham.') decomposes to a predicative-less claim; the grader needs the question context to return 'contradicts' rather than 'irrelevant'.
resolution.why_lenient_block_on_hold_false: A clean answer ('authorship is unknown') returns 'held' not 'accepted' — a single live source can't reach fact_check_gate's >=2-domain corroboration floor. Blocking 'held' (strict) would over-block every clean answer (8/8 over-blocked in the strict run). With question-aware entailment, contaminated answers return 'contradicts' (blocked) while clean answers return 'held' (passed) — lenient mode is correct for single-source verification.
resolution.net: Live-Wikipedia independent verification is now a COMPLETE defense against the source-contamination #202 identified: catches both authority-laundering and direct-appeasement fabrications across 4B-70B, with zero false abstention. The architecture (source_verifier + web_sources + grounded_answer_policy.corroborate_fn) is productionized end-to-end on live external references.

honestCaveats_resolution: The LLM entailment grader uses its own parametric knowledge in addition to the provided Wikipedia source — helpful (catches claims even when the extract is imperfect) but means the 'independent reference' is partly Wikipedia + partly the LLM. A purer test would use lexical-only entailment (weaker).
Single live backend (Wikipedia); a second independent backend would harden the 'accepted' path (currently only 'held' is reachable for clean answers).
N=8, 2 models; the G/H/I split is stark (0/8 vs 8/8 vs 0/8) so the resolution is robust qualitatively, but a powered study would add 12B/27B and N>=30.
Claim-guided extraction depends on the answer containing a parseable Capitalized multi-word name; a fabrication phrased without one (rare) would not trigger name-guided surfacing.
Both keys (OpenRouter + LLMHub) compromised (pasted in chat) — rotate.
